description: POST /sessions/{name}/exec. TODAY: prefix-less `<json>\n\n` frames mislabeled text/event-stream — a WHATWG parser misreads the JSON's first `key:` as an (unknown, ignored) SSE field, recovering EMPTY data. TARGET (Phase 1, LIVE): coord re-frames each execd event as a real `data:` SSE frame (execd vocabulary: init/stdout/stderr/execution_complete/error; the event's own `type` stays the discriminator). The empty-data today-frames are the migration proof.

vectors.name: today-happy
vectors.note: bare JSON; WHATWG recovers empty data (the lie). The Ruby EventStreamParser tolerates it via its bare-JSON fallback; a strict WHATWG parser cannot.

vectors.name: target-happy
vectors.note: coord re-frames each execd event as a data: frame; execution_complete is execd's terminal event

vectors.name: target-keepalive
vectors.note: the `: ka` keep-alive comment between frames is dropped (no field).
